project: a daily generative art practice, after Zach Lieberman
batch_note: Argentina at the World Cup 2026 — one piece per match.

sketches.title: Terreno
sketches.subject: Argentina vs. Algeria
sketches.note: Match pressure surveyed as terrain: bone-white contours floating on the dark room, drawn once. The three summits are Messi's 17', 60', 76' — his first World Cup hat-trick; Algeria's side stays low ground.

sketches.title: Sismógrafo
sketches.subject: Argentina vs. Austria
sketches.note: A plotter's seismogram of the ninety-six minutes on a deckle-edged strip; the bookings cut below the line, and the record ends on its deepest spike — Messi's 90+5' in gold, the all-time World Cup scoring record.

sketches.title: Resonancia
sketches.subject: Jordan vs. Argentina
sketches.note: Four Chladni resonances, one per goal — the penalty snaps, Al-Tamari's 55' inks its figure kit-red. The cycle runs again and again, the whole figure turning clockwise, one revolution every two and a half minutes.

sketches.title: Bronce
sketches.subject: Argentina vs. Cabo Verde
sketches.note: A commemorative plaque, sand-cast in bronze; one raking lamp settles, the patina keeps the score.

sketches.title: Embate
sketches.subject: Argentina vs. Egypt
sketches.medium: two-dye fluid collision · gpu stable fluids
sketches.note: Two fronts on the match's clock — Egypt's ochre holds the line for an hour, the celeste breaks through at 90+2', and the waters regather to clash again.

sketches.title: Afiche
sketches.subject: Argentina vs. Switzerland
sketches.note: The matchday poster Switzerland's own typographic tradition would print, mailed folded to eighths: it arrives as a packet wearing both federations' shields and opens fold by fold, every plate a breath out of register. Ninety minutes scoreless — la prórroga gives it 3–1. Tap, and the eleven walk back to kickoff; tap again, the drift replays.

sketches.title: Umbral
sketches.subject: England vs. Argentina
sketches.note: The two captains as one portrait typeset from the match's own record, the shadow left unprinted. England lead through Gordon at 55′; Messi turns it late with both assists — Enzo Fernández 85′, Lautaro 90+2′. Tap, and the portrait swells into the other captain; drag, and a torch wakes the numbers hiding in the black.
